name: Flatex Degiro
description: Investigative forensic dossier pending high-fidelity injection.
fullReview:
# flatexDEGIRO AG (flatex + DEGIRO) — Comprehensive Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026  
**Target:** flatexDEGIRO AG (flatexdegiro.com) — Parent of flatex and DEGIRO brands  
**Sources:** Wikipedia, flatexDEGIRO AG public filings, BaFin, AFM/DNB, FCA registers, TrustPilot, financial reports, industry data

---

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

flatexDEGIRO AG is a **publicly listed German financial technology company** based in **Frankfurt am Main, Germany**. The company is the parent of two major online brokerage brands — **flatex** (German market focus) and **DEGIRO** (pan-European market focus). Understanding the combined entity is essential because all regulatory coverage, financial resources, and operational infrastructure flow through flatexDEGIRO AG as the holding company.

#### flatex Heritage (1999–2020)

The flatex brand traces its origins to **July 1999**, when it was founded as **PRE.IPO AG** in Frankfurt, Germany. The name "flatex" is a portmanteau derived from the English words **"flat"** (meaning all-inclusive, flat-rate pricing) and **"execution"** (trade execution) — the customer pays a fixed amount per securities transaction regardless of volume.

flatex entered the online brokerage market in **2006** and quickly established itself as one of Germany's leading direct stock brokers. Key milestones in the flatex pre-merger history include:

- **2006:** flatex brand launched as online discount broker
- **2007:** Obtained full German banking license (Kreditwesengesetz — KWG) from BaFin — a significant differentiator from most online brokers
- **2009:** IPO as Flatex AG; co-founding involvement of Bernd Förtsch, publisher of "Der Aktionär" stock market newsletter
- **2014:** Renamed to **FinTech Group AG** as the company expanded its fintech ambitions
- **March 2015:** Acquired majority stake in **XCOM AG** (technology/software company founded 1988)
- **August 2017:** XCOM AG merged with Flatex AG, transferring what is now flatexDEGIRO Bank AG into the group
- **2017:** Named **"Bank of the Year"** by the Oskar Patzelt Foundation
- **August 2019:** Renamed back to **Flatex AG**

During this period, flatex built its reputation on:
- Ultra-low stock and ETF commissions
- Comprehensive German tax automation (automatic tax withholding and reporting — a major value add for German investors)
- Strong BaFin regulatory framework with full banking license
- German-engineered platform reliability

#### DEGIRO Heritage (2008–2020)

DEGIRO was founded in **2008** by five former BinckBank employees, initially serving professional traders. Retail services launched on **September 23, 2013** in the Netherlands. DEGIRO rapidly expanded pan-Europe with:

- Commission starting at €1 per trade (revolutionary for European retail market)
- Commission-free ETF selection
- Access to 50,000+ instruments across 50+ global exchanges
- Clean, mobile-first design

#### The 2019–2020 Merger

In **December 2019**, Flatex AG announced the acquisition of **100% of DEGIRO shares**. The transaction was completed in **July 2020**, creating the largest online brokerage group in Europe at the time. The merger was strategic:

- flatex contributed: German market dominance, full German banking license, tax automation expertise
- DEGIRO contributed: Pan-European reach, 50,000+ instrument range, technology-first design
- **Both brands retained their identity** under the dual-brand holding structure
- The combined entity was renamed **flatexDEGIRO AG** at the October 2020 Annual General Meeting
- Added to the **Frankfurt Stock Exchange Prime Standard** (October 2020), followed by **SDAX inclusion** (two months later)

At the time of merger, flatexDEGIRO projected it would serve over one million customers executing 35+ million transactions annually with €200+ billion in transaction volume.

## 2. REGULATION — EVERY LICENSE

### 2.1 Germany — BaFin (Top-Tier Banking License)

| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Regulator** | Bundesanstalt für Finanzdienstleistungsaufsicht (BaFin) |
| **Entity: flatex** | flatexDEGIRO Bank AG — Full German banking license (Kreditwesengesetz — KWG) |
| **Entity: Holding** | flatexDEGIRO AG — HRB 105192 (Frankfurt commercial register) |
| **Regulatory Tier** | **Tier 1 — Highest (Full bank license)** |
| **Deposit Protection** | German statutory deposit guarantee (EdB) up to **€100,000** per client |
| **Investor Compensation** | Up to €20,000 (statutory EU standard) |

**Significance of Banking License:** flatexDEGIRO Bank AG holds a full German banking license under KWG — not merely an investment services license. This means:
- Client cash deposits are protected up to €100,000 under the statutory German deposit guarantee scheme — significantly higher than the standard €20,000 investment compensation
- The bank is subject to BaFin's most stringent capital adequacy and liquidity requirements
- Quarterly BaFin reporting, independent audits, and corporate governance standards apply
- This is among the strongest regulatory protections available to European retail brokerage clients

### 2.2 Germany — BaFin (Enforcement Actions)

| Date | Action | Amount |
|------|--------|--------|
| **Sep 8, 2022** | Additional capital requirements imposed on flatexDEGIRO Bank AG and parent company | Undisclosed additional capital |
| **Feb 7, 2023** | **BaFin fine imposed** on flatexDEGIRO Bank AG for banking supervisory regulation violations | **€1,050,000** |
| **Feb 21, 2023** | Measures became legally final | Confirmed |

The BaFin enforcement action (finalized February 21, 2023) represents a significant regulatory event for the company. BaFin found that flatexDEGIRO Bank AG had violated banking supervisory regulations and imposed both a monetary penalty and additional capital requirements on both the bank and the parent holding group. These measures underscore the importance of monitoring the group's ongoing compliance trajectory.

### 2.3 Netherlands — AFM + DNB

| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Regulator: Conduct** | Autoriteit Financiële Markten (AFM) |
| **Regulator: Prudential** | De Nederlandsche Bank (DNB) |
| **Entity** | DEGIRO B.V. (subsidiary of flatexDEGIRO AG) |
| **Regulatory Tier** | **Tier 1** |
| **Protections** | Dutch investor compensation up to €20,000 |

**Note:** DNB took formal action against DEGIRO in **December 2021** regarding IT infrastructure stability, ordering a €3M+ additional capital reserve (detailed in DEGIRO-specific regulatory section).

### 2.4 United Kingdom — FCA

| **Entity** | DEGIRO UK (Dairycrest Ltd / DEGIRO UK entity) |
| **FRN** | **684312** |
| **Regulatory Tier** | **Tier 1** |
| **Compensation** | Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) — up to £85,000 |

### 2.5 EU Cross-Border Passporting

DEGIRO operates across multiple European jurisdictions through MiFID II passporting from its AFM license.

## 3. TRADING CONDITIONS — COMPLETE

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: NOT FOREX BROKERS

Both flatex and DEGIRO offer **stock and ETF investment products only**. Neither offers:
- ❌ Forex (FX) trading
- ❌ CFDs (Contracts for Difference)
- ❌ Leveraged/margin products
- ❌ Spread betting
- ❌ Binary options
- ❌ Cryptocurrency trading

## 4. PLATFORMS — EVERYTHING

### 4.1 flatex Trading Platform

flatex provides a **web-based trading platform and desktop application** designed specifically for the German market:

**Core Features:**
- Order management and placement
- Portfolio overview and performance tracking
- Comprehensive tax reporting (German tax automation — unique advantage)
- Savings plan (Sparplan) management
- Extensive German market data feeds
- German-language interface (primary; limited English)

**Strengths:**
- **Automatic German tax reporting** — flatex handles all KapErtragSt (capital gains tax) withholding and reporting automatically, a significant convenience for German tax residents
- Reliable, German-engineered platform stability
- Extensive savings plan functionality

**Limitations:**
- Language primarily German — limited English support for international users
- Functional but not modern design aesthetic

### 4.2 flatex Mobile App

Available on iOS and Android:

**Features:**
- Portfolio management
- Order placement
- Savings plan management
- Account overview

**User Feedback:**
- App Store rating approximately 3.8+ (lower than DEGIRO's)
- Some users have requested English language support
- Reviews note both positive experiences and occasional administration issues

### 4.3 DEGIRO Trading Platform

DEGIRO offers a **proprietary web platform and mobile app** (iOS/Android):

**Core Features:**
- Clean, minimalist dashboard
- Real-time position tracking with P&L
- Order types: Market, Limit, Stop, Stop-Limit, Trailing Stop
- Multi-exchange routing with price comparison
- Watchlist management
- Corporate action notifications
- Dividend income tracking
- Portfolio performance analysis

**Mobile App:**
- Full trading parity with web platform
- Biometric login (Face ID / fingerprint)
- Push notifications
- Generally rated 4.2+ on App Store, 4.0+ on Google Play

### 4.4 Platform Integration Summary

| Feature | flatex | DEGIRO |

---

## 5. USER REVIEWS — AGGREGATED

### 5.1 flatex TrustPilot (www.flatex.de)

**Overall Sentiment: Mixed-to-Positive** — Reviews span from highly positive to severely negative

**Positive Themes:**
- **"Excellent client service"** — One user reported immediate phone support, even outside office hours, with a representative who helped reinstall the app without being asked
- **Security features** praised: "Flatex is really secure as it has enabled two-factor authentication"
- **Fast, precise service** noted: "They are always very precise and quick, understand the importance of time and money"
- **VIP customer support** mentioned by some users as responsive
- **German tax automation** consistently praised as a major advantage for German investors

**Negative Themes:**
- **Existing customer neglect:** One long-standing customer complained about flatex offering **€500 credit in order commissions for new customers** while ignoring existing customers, calling it "disappointing" and noting that "existing customers are better left ignored"
- **Account migration problems:** One user reported trying to move their account from Germany to Austria for **10 months** with no resolution ("Ten. Months... radio silence for ages. Still nothing. Unresolved.")
- **Administration errors:** "They can't recognize a signature... I lost money due to this"
- **Forced liquidation complaints:** One user described what they termed "coercion and illegal account access" when flatex reversed a purchase and demanded cover of a short position within 45 minutes
- **Fee complaints:** "Horrible fees... rip-off fee structures" — particularly from users who experienced errors that resulted in charges (€64 internal transfer fee mentioned)
- **Language barrier:** Multiple international users (Austria, UK) noted lack of English support for the flatex app
- **Hostile UI** complaints from users migrating from DEGIRO to flatex: "Most user-hostile interface... horrible app, unclear"
- **Delayed money transfers:** "Takes 2-4 days for them to transfer money back to my bank account" (though flatex responded that transfers are usually within one bank working day)

### 5.2 DEGIRO TrustPilot (referenced from degiro report)

DEGIRO reviews are generally more positive with core themes around:
- Low cost praise ("Fast, simple and cheap")
- Reliable platform for long-term investing
- Customer support limitations during peak periods

---

## 6. COMPETITIVE POSITION

### 6.1 flatexDEGIRO Group vs. European Competitors

| Feature | flatexDEGIRO | Trading 212 | TradeRepublic | Interactive Brokers | Scalable Capital |
|---------|-------------|-------------|---------------|--------------------|-----------------|
| **Legal Structure** | Full bank + broker | Broker | Neobank/broker | Global broker | Robo + broker |
| **Stock Commission** | €1.50–€5.90+ (flatex); €1+ (DEGIRO) | £0 (spread) | €1 | $0.005/share | €0.99–€4.99 |
| **ETF Savings Plans** | €1.50 or free | Free pies | Free select | ✅ Available | Free select |
| **Deposit Protection** | €100,000 (flatex bank) | FSCS £85K | €100K | SIPC $500K | €100K |
| **Publicly Listed** | ✅ SDAX: FTG | ❌ Private | ❌ Private | ✅ NASDAQ: IBKR | ❌ Private |
| **Global Reach** | 14+ countries | UK/EU/AU | Germany + EU | 200+ markets | Germany focus |
| **Tax Automation** | ✅ German market leader | Limited | Growing | Complex | German focus |
| **Forex/CFDs** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | ✅ Yes | ❌ No |

### 6.2 Unique Selling Propositions

1. **Full German banking license** — Among European online brokers, flatexDEGIRO's banking license (providing €100,000 deposit protection vs. the standard €20,000 investment compensation) is a major structural advantage
2. **SDAX-listed transparency** — Public quarterly financials, independent audits, German governance standards
3. **Combined scale** — 2M+ clients, 1,250 employees, €480M revenue (2024) — among the largest European pure-play online brokerages
4. **German tax automation leadership** — flatex's automatic KapErtragSt handling is unmatched among competitors for German tax residents
5. **Dual-brand strategy** — flatex for Germany, DEGIRO for pan-Europe — allows targeting different segments with tailored propositions
6. **Multi-regulatory compliance** — BaFin + AFM/DNB + FCA coverage

### 6.3 Structural Weaknesses

1. **No forex/CFD offering** — Misses the entire retail FX trading market
2. **No leverage products** — Cannot serve margin traders
3. **No API access** — Loses algorithmic traders to Interactive Brokers
4. **flatex English language gap** — International users in German-speaking markets face language barriers
5. **Customer support inconsistency** — TrustPilot reviews show wide variance between excellent support and "radio silence for weeks"
6. **Post-merger integration complexity** — Combining flatex and DEGIRO into a unified organization remains ongoing
7. **Regulatory enforcement history** — BaFin 2023 fine (€1.05M) and DNB 2021 capital order are regulatory red flags

### 6.4 Market Position

flatexDEGIRO AG occupies the **leading European retail online brokerage** position by revenue and client base among SDAX-listed financial technology companies. The group is the largest pure-play online broker in the DACH region and one of the top three pan-European retail brokerages by client count. The dual-brand strategy enables market coverage of both the mature German market (flatex) and the growing pan-European market (DEGIRO).

---

## 7. FINANCIAL DATA

### 7.1 flatexDEGIRO AG — Public Financial Performance (SDAX: FTG)

As an SDAX-listed company, flatexDEGIRO AG publishes detailed quarterly and annual financial results. The following data is sourced from the company's public filings:

| Metric | 2024 | 2023 | 2021 (Peak) |
|--------|------|------|--------------|
| **Revenue** | **€480,024,000** | €390,732,000 | ~€200M+ |
| **Operating Income (EBIT)** | **€155,652,000** | €103,016,000 | — |
| **Net Income** | **€111,535,000** | €71,859,000 | €70M+ |
| **Clients** | 2M+ | 2M+ | ~2M |
| **Securities Transactions** | — | — | **91 million (2021)** |
| **Employees** | **1,250** | — | — |

**Key Financial Observations:**
1. **Revenue growth:** 2024 revenue of €480M represents approximately **23% year-over-year growth** vs. 2023 (€390.7M)
2. **Profit margin improvement:** Operating income grew **51% YoY** (€103M → €155.6M), reflecting significant operating leverage and cost efficiencies
3. **Net income growth:** Net income increased **55% YoY** (€71.9M → €111.5M), demonstrating strong bottom-line improvement
4. **2021 was a peak year** for transaction volume (91M securities transactions) driven by the pandemic-era retail investing boom
5. The company has demonstrated resilience in post-boom normalization, with 2024 showing strong recovery

**Revenue Model Breakdown:**
- Trading commissions (primary revenue driver)
- Payment for Order Flow (PFOF) via Tradegate routing (DEGIRO brand)
- Currency conversion spreads and fees
- Custody/settlement services
- Savings plan fees

---

## 8. AFFILIATE PROGRAMS

### Status: **Not Available for Both Brands**

**flatex:** No affiliate, Introducing Broker (IB), CPA, or revenue-share program identified. flatex focuses on organic growth within the German market through:
- Direct marketing and brand awareness
- Referral bonuses (occasional €500 order commission credit for new customers — though this has been a customer complaint driver when existing clients are excluded)
- "Der Aktionär" publication relationship (founder Bernd Förtsch)

**DEGIRO:** No traditional affiliate program. Marketing focuses on commission-free ETF value proposition and pan-European brand awareness.

**Implications:** Neither brand provides affiliate revenue opportunities for marketers. Forex/CFD brokers with established IB programs offer significantly more lucrative affiliate partnerships.

---

## 9. RISK ASSESSMENT

### 9.1 Regulatory Risk Profile

| Factor | Assessment | Detail |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| **Full Banking License** | ✅✅ Excellent | BaFin KWG license — highest EU standard |
| **Multi-Regulator** | ✅✅ Excellent | BaFin + AFM/DNB + FCA |
| **SDAX-Listed Transparency** | ✅ Excellent | Regular public financial reporting |
| **Deposit Protection** | ✅✅ Excellent | €100,000 (flatex) vs. standard €20,000 |
| **BaFin Enforcement** | ⚠️ Concern | €1.05M fine (Feb 2023) |
| **Capital Requirements** | ⚠️ Concern | Additional capital mandated Sep 2022 |
| **DNB IT Action** | ⚠️ Concern | €3M+ reserve ordered (Dec 2021) |

### 9.2 Operational Risk

| Factor | Assessment | Detail |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| **Revenue Resilience** | ✅ Good | €480M 2024 revenue (post-boom recovery) |
| **Client Base Stability** | ✅ Good | 2M+ clients; scale advantage |
| **Post-Merger Integration** | ⚠️ Ongoing | Dual brand complexity; 4+ years in |
| **Customer Support Quality** | ⚠️ Variable | Wide TrustPilot variance |
| **Technology Platform** | ✅ Adequate | 1,250 employees supporting |
| **No Leverage Products** | ✅ Lower Risk | Cash-only investing |

### 9.3 Key Risk Factors

1. **EU PFOF Ban Risk:** European Commission discussions on PFOF prohibition could eliminate a significant DEGIRO revenue stream (Tradegate routing)
2. **Regulatory Compliance Trajectory:** BaFin 2023 fine + DNB 2021 enforcement = pattern of regulatory scrutiny
3. **Client Churn to Zero-Commission Competitors:** TradeRepublic and Trading 212 offer free trading that undercuts flatex's €5.90 XETRA pricing
4. **Concentration Risk:** Revenue heavily dependent on retail trading activity levels (cyclical)
5. **Integration Complexity:** Managing flatex (Germany) + DEGIRO (pan-Europe) under one corporate structure creates operational complexity

### 9.4 Overall Risk Rating: MODERATE-LOW

The full banking license, SDAX-listed transparency, and €480M revenue provide strong structural stability. However, the regulatory enforcement history and PFOF dependency add medium-level risk elements. The overall profile is stronger than most non-bank online brokers but below the risk profile of larger, globally diversified firms like Interactive Brokers.

---

## 10. OVERALL ASSESSMENT

### 10.1 Broker Group Profile

flatexDEGIRO AG is **Europe's largest publicly-listed pure-play online brokerage group** by revenue (€480M, 2024) and among the top three by client count (2M+). The group operates two distinct yet complementary brands:

- **flatex** — German market focus with full banking license, tax automation, and strong domestic market position
- **DEGIRO** — Pan-European focus with lowest-cost positioning, 50,000+ instruments across 50+ exchanges

For pure **stock and ETF investing** within Europe, flatexDEGIRO represents one of the most established, well-capitalized, and well-regulated options available.

**Ideal for:**
- German retail investors seeking integrated tax handling
- European buy-and-hold equity investors
- Cost-conscious investors prioritizing low commissions
- Investors valuing banking-level deposit protection (flatex)
- Long-term portfolio builders

**Not suitable for:**
- Forex/CFD traders (no FX products)
- Day traders or high-frequency traders
- Algorithmic traders (no API)
- Investors seeking leverage or margin
- Active traders requiring advanced charting tools

### 10.2 Final Scoring

| Category | Score | Rationale |
|----------|-------|-----------|
| **Regulation** | **9/10** | BaFin banking license + AFM/DNB + FCA; demerit for BaFin fine |
| **Trading Conditions** | **N/A** (not a forex broker) | — |
| **Platforms** | **7/10** | Functional for both brands; DEGIRO superior UX; flatex English gap |
| **User Experience** | **7.5/10** | Strong overall; support quality variable |
| **Financial Strength** | **9/10** | €480M revenue, €111M net income, SDAX-listed, profitable |
| **Deposits/Withdrawals** | **8/10** | Free SEPA, instant options; flatex 2-4 day complaints |
| **Customer Support** | **6/10** | Wide variance (excellent to "radio silence for weeks") |
| **Cost Competitiveness** | **8.5/10** | DEGIRO very competitive; flatex moderate |
| **Trust/Reputation** | **7/10** | Banking license builds trust; enforcement actions detract |
| **Awards** | **4/10** | "Bank of the Year" 2017; otherwise limited |
| **Education** | **6/10** | Basic; flatex tax guides; DEGIRO onboarding course (UK) |
| **OVERALL** | **7/10** *(as equity investment broker)* | Leading European online brokerage group with strong fundamentals |

### 10.3 Bottom Line

flatexDEGIRO AG is the **largest publicly-listed European online brokerage group** with a full banking license, €480M in annual revenue, and a dual-brand strategy covering both the German domestic market and the broader European landscape. The combined flatex + DEGIRO offering provides comprehensive, low-cost equity investing with top-tier regulatory protection. While not relevant for forex/CFD traders, it represents a gold-standard option for European retail equity investors who prioritize safety, cost-efficiency, and regulatory oversight.

## 11. WITHDRAWAL SPEED & METHODS

### 11.1 Supported Methods

| Method | Processing Time | Fees | Brand |
|--------|----------------|------|-------|
| **SEPA Transfer** | 1 business day (flatex response standard) | FREE | Both |
| **SEPA Instant Transfer** | Minutes (where bank supports) | FREE | Both |
| **Wire (Non-SEPA)** | 3-5 business days | Variable | Both |

### 11.2 Withdrawal Process

- Withdrawals must go to the **same bank account** used for deposits (AML requirement)
- Standard SEPA withdrawal processed within **one bank working day** per flatex policy
- Weekend/holiday processing can extend timelines (no booking runs on weekends)
- User feedback is mixed: some report same-day transfers, others report 2-4 day waits

### 11.3 TrustPilot Withdrawal Feedback

- Complaint: "Takes 2-4 days for them to transfer money back to my bank account"
- flatex Response: "Transfers are usually made within one bank working day. Please take booking times into account... no booking runs at the weekend"
- Assessment: Standard EU transfer timelines; no systemic withdrawal issues identified

### 11.4 Currency Conversion on Withdrawals

DEGIRO clients withdrawing in non-EUR currencies face the €10 conversion fee + 0.25% FX spread. This is a well-documented friction point, particularly for clients in Poland, Switzerland, and the UK.

## 14. CHARITY & CSR

### Corporate Social Responsibility

As an SDAX-listed German company, flatexDEGIRO AG is subject to German sustainability reporting requirements (CSR-Richtlinie-Umsetzungsgesetz). CSR reporting would be included in the annual management report:

**Expected CSR Focus Areas:**
- **Financial inclusion:** Democratizing access to equity markets for European retail investors
- **Digital financial services innovation:** Technology-first approach reducing investing barriers
- **Corporate governance:** German two-tier board structure, SDAX compliance
- **Employee base:** 1,250 employees across 14+ locations in DE, NL, AT, BG
- **Sustainability/Green investing:** Growing ESG ETF offerings through both platforms

**Limitations:**
- No major public charitable partnerships or foundation activities identified under the flatexDEGIRO brand
- CSR efforts would be reported through parent company annual reports
- BaFin enforcement history (2023 fine) may impact CSR positioning

flatexDEGIRO's award profile is weighted toward the **German domestic market** rather than international recognition. ### DEGIRO Promotions

| Offer | Status | Details |
|-------|--------|---------|
| **Commission-Free Core ETF Selection** | ✅ Core offering | ~200+ ETFs at €0 |
| **€0 Minimum Deposit** | ✅ Always | Not a time-limited promotion |
| **Free SEPA Transfers** | ✅ Standard | No fees on deposits/withdrawals |
| **New Account Promotions** | Occasional | €10–€20 credit during specific periods |

### Promotion Philosophy

flatexDEGIRO competes on **structural value** (low ongoing costs, banking protection) rather than aggressive promotional acquisition. The €500 flatex new-client offer is the most substantial promotion identified but has drawn criticism for excluding existing customers.

### No Affiliate Promotion Programs

Neither flatex nor DEGIRO operates affiliate/IB programs offering commissions to referrers, CPA deals, or revenue-share arrangements for marketers.
